task: openclaw-benben vNext local lane streaming repair

problem.symptom: Feishu /lite entered local lane, but ordinary local Qwen replies could wait until the 120s adapter timeout and return the generic unavailable text.
problem.root_cause: The local lane called the NAS Ollama relay with stream=false and waited for response.json(). Evidence showed the relay could deliver generated JSON content but keep the HTTP response open long enough for clients to time out.
problem.rejected_fix: Do not solve this by forcing tiny 160-256 token answers; that makes /lite too low-value for normal use.

changes.summary: Changed the default local Ollama adapter to request stream=true and parse NDJSON chunks until done=true, then cancel the reader and return accumulated content.
Added normal-length prediction budgets: /lite 1536 tokens and /gemma 1024 tokens. These are output guardrails, not short-answer caps.
Added local-lane thinking cleanup for <think> blocks, Thinking... prefixes, and visible thinking-process headers before Feishu delivery.
Added regression coverage for streaming Ollama requests, normal prediction budget, and thinking text cleanup.
Synced the patched plugin source to the shadow state root and restarted only openclaw-benben.service.

rollback.strategy: Restore the backed-up plugin index.js to /var/lib/openclaw-benben/.openclaw/workspace/plugins/openclaw-benben-vnext/index.js and restart openclaw-benben.service. Old production openclaw-gateway.service was not modified.

shadow_checks: remote sha256 matches local plugin source
real shadow adapter call through http://127.0.0.1:11435 returned 618 Chinese characters in 16272ms with hasThinking=false

residual_risk: Feishu still receives final text after the model finishes; this repair does not implement incremental Feishu streaming.
Very long prompts can still take tens of seconds on Qwen 9B, but completed relay responses no longer wait for the relay connection to close naturally.
The live shadow config still contains timeoutMs=120000 for /lite and gemma timeoutMs=45000; those remain as upper bounds.
